meaning: n.(剧院雇用的)拍手喝彩者,一群捧场者
examples: Claque: a group of persons hired to applaud at a performance.
But, just as he did during the campaign, Obama has relied on only a tiny claque of trusted aides for advice on the major decisions confronting him.
He threw financial prudence out the window in order to appease his business cronies and faithful media claque, particularly those working for mainstream eastern media.
